title: Example 1: Frontend Design
description: A three-step walkthrough showing how the frontend-design plugin works end to end — from install command to production UI output. Each step is a distinct visual panel.

parts.title: Install the Plugin
parts.description: A terminal window showing the install command: claude plugin add anthropics/claude-plugins-official frontend-design. Below the command, a file tree expands showing three new items dropped into the project: CLAUDE.md (design conventions), frontend-design SKILL.md, and a Figma MCP server config file.

parts.title: The Skill Runs
parts.description: A split panel: on the left, the frontend-design SKILL.md file open showing design system rules, component structure guidelines, and styling conventions. On the right, Claude reading the file before writing any code — represented by an eye icon scanning the document. Annotations highlight: No ad hoc decisions · No style drift.

parts.title: The Output
parts.description: A rendered UI component preview showing a clean, production-grade card or form element with correct structure, consistent styling, and accessible markup. A label beneath reads: 'Built in one pass.' Two attribution tags: 'The skill did the thinking. Claude did the work.'
